summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint on install
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js harvests the developer's identity and sends it to an attacker-controlled endpoint. The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's git email and GitHub username/email, collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, current working directory, and CI provider, then POSTs the JSON payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ via https.request (postinstall.js line 161, line 165). The README falsely claims telemetry is limited to 'platform, Node version, architecture' — the actual payload is materially broader and targets developer-identity files that are not legitimate telemetry sources. The package additionally impersonates OpenAI's official `@openai/agents` SDK: name `openai-agents-helpers`, author 'OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>', and homepage `https://openai-agents-js.guide` are crafted to appear officially blessed, and the README mimics the official SDK's API surface to lure installs. Installing this package leaks the developer's GitHub identity, git email, machine hostname, OS username, and project path to the attacker, enabling targeted phishing, account linking, and further supply-chain pivots.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
